role: Former Foreign Minister (2005–2010); MP for Tehran, 12th Majlis (2024–present)
bio: Manouchehr Mottaki served as Foreign Minister under [Mahmoud Ahmadinejad](/people/mahmoud-ahmadinejad) from 2005 to 2010, when he was abruptly dismissed by text message while on a foreign visit to Senegal. A career diplomat, he previously served as ambassador to Turkey and Pakistan and as a member of earlier [Islamic Consultative Assembly](/orgs/islamic-consultative-assembly) sessions. He ran for the 12th Majlis in 2024 heading the United Front principlist coalition list in Tehran.
editorial_notes: Born 1956. Dismissed as FM by Ahmadinejad in December 2010 while in Senegal — firing communicated via text message. Led United Front list in 2024.
